name: Old White Cut Pork Slices with Garlic Sesame
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 lb boneless pork leg (butt or loin)
1/4 cup Shao-Hsing rice wine or dry sherry
1/2 tsp hot pepper oil or to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Fresh ham or a fairly fat cut of pork is traditionally used for this recipe, but boneless lean pork loin makes a delicious and leaner substitute.
2. For a nontraditional presentation, serve the pork with grilled asparagus spears, Asian eggplant and/or zucchini slices. Alternatively, arrange the slices on a bed of watercress with strips of roasted pepper and serve as a first course salad. Or, simply alternate the pork with thin rounds of chilled cucumber.
3. Bring the water to a boil in a large pot. Add the pork, green onions, ginger, garlic, and wine. Return to a boil. Skim off any scum that rises to the surface.
4. Cover, reduce heat to low, and simmer for 45 minutes. When the meat is done, half fill a large pan with cold water and ice cubes. Remove the meat from the pot and immediately plunge it into the ice water; let sit for 20 minutes to firm up the meat and juices. Remove pork, pat dry, cover, and refrigerate until thoroughly chilled, at least 2 to 3 hours or overnight.
5. To serve: Cut the meat crosswise into paper-thin slices (no thicker than 1/8 inch) and arrange in a circular pattern on a plate. Serve sauce on the side or drizzle it over the pork. Garnish with fresh coriander sprigs.
